Listen & Identify
Recognition via
— tap ⚙️ to choose —
Hold your phone near the speakers
⚙️ Choose a recognition service first
🎙️ Microphone blocked? Fix it here ▼
📱 Android — Chrome browser
1. Tap the lock icon 🔒 in Chrome's address bar
2. Tap Permissions
3. Find Microphone → switch to Allow
4. Refresh this page and tap the mic button again
🍎 iPhone — Safari
1. Open iPhone Settings
2. Scroll down → tap Safari
3. Tap Microphone → set to Allow
4. Return to this page and try the mic button
No mic? The 🔍 Search button fills in all the same fields without needing a microphone.
Identified — tap to add to collection
🎤 Recognition Service
All free · Choose any · Keys stay on your device
🎵
AudD FREE
300 free requests/account · No HMAC · Simple token · 160M track DB · Returns artist, album, release date, Spotify/Apple links
dashboard.audd.io → sign up → copy token
✓
1. Go to dashboard.audd.io → sign up free
2. Copy your API token
3. Paste below
📡
Shazam (RapidAPI) FREE TIER
500 free requests/month via RapidAPI · World's largest recognition DB · Apple-owned · Extremely accurate for popular music
rapidapi.com → search "Shazam" → subscribe free → copy X-RapidAPI-Key
✓
1. Go to rapidapi.com → create free account
2. Search "Shazam" → subscribe to free plan (500 req/mo)
3. Copy your X-RapidAPI-Key from the API console
4. Paste below
🔊
ACRCloud FREE TIER
100 free requests/day · Industry standard · Powers Shazam competitors · Requires Host + Key + Secret · HMAC-SHA1 signing
acrcloud.com → sign up → create project → copy 3 credentials
✓
1. Go to acrcloud.com → sign up free
2. Create a project → copy Host, Access Key, Access Secret
3. Paste all three below
🏷️
AudioTag COMPLETELY FREE
No account required · 46M+ tracks · Upload audio file · Proprietary fingerprinting since 2009 · No API key needed at all
audiotag.info — no sign-up, no limits stated, no key
✓
🎉
No setup needed!
AudioTag requires no account, no API key, and no registration. Just tap the mic and go. Note: sends audio to audiotag.info — no personal data, audio is deleted after fingerprinting.
⚡
AudD (No Key) NO SIGN-UP
10 requests/day with no account or key — perfect for occasional use or testing. Same AudD engine, just rate-limited. Zero setup.
api.audd.io — no key required, just send audio
✓
🚀
Zero setup!
AudD allows 10 free recognitions per day with no account needed. Great for casual use — no key, no sign-up, works right now.
🎶
Hum to Search FREE
Hum, whistle, or sing the melody — AudD's humming recognition will find the song. Uses AudD token (same as option 1). Works even if you only half-remember the tune.
Requires AudD token (same key as option 1 above)
✓
🎶
Hum the melody!
Uses the same AudD token. If you've already saved it in option 1, you're ready. Otherwise enter your AudD token in option 1 first.

Pricing Currency
All Discogs prices and buy links will use this currency. Default is USD.
💡 For buy links, also set your currency in Discogs → Settings → Currency to match — your account preference can override the URL.
Shipping Country
Used to calculate total cost including shipping. Prices shown are cheapest delivered to your country.
Tap a currency to select — saves automatically
Import from Discogs
How to export from Discogs
1
Go to discogs.com and log in to your account
2
Go to My Collection (click your username → Collection)
3
Click Export (top right of collection page) → Export to CSV
4
Wait for the email from Discogs with a download link, download the .csv file
5
Come back here and tap Choose CSV File below
Import Settings
If a record already exists in your collection:
Choose Your CSV File
Processing…
What gets imported▼
Artist → Artist
Title → Album Title
Label → Label
Format → Format
Released → Year
Catalog# → Cat No (Edition)
release_id → Discogs ID
Rating → Rating (1–5)
Media Condition → Vinyl Condition
Sleeve Condition → Sleeve Condition
Date Added → Date Bought
Notes → Notes

Market Value

Find out what your records are worth & when to sell

📖 How This Works
▼ tap to expand
This module helps you figure out the real market price of any record you own and gives you a condition-adjusted sell price based on your vinyl's grade. It uses the same pricing logic as Discogs dealers.
1
Pick a record from your collection
Use the dropdown above to select which LP you want to evaluate.
2
Look up the Discogs median price
Go to discogs.com → search your album → click it → scroll to Statistics → copy the Median price (last 90 days sold).
3
Paste it in and get your price instantly
We apply the standard condition multiplier for your vinyl grade (VG+, VG, etc.) and show you a suggested sell price, profit, ROI, and whether now is a good time to sell.
4
Save it to your record
Tap Save Market Value to update that record's current value in your collection and log it in the price history.
Set Up Discogs & API Keys
▼ tap to expand
Why Discogs? Discogs is the world's largest vinyl marketplace with real sold-price data for millions of releases. Their median price (last 90 days of actual sales) is the most accurate valuation tool available for records.
Step 1 — Create a free Discogs account
1
Go to discogs.com on your phone or computer
2
Tap Register (top right) → enter email and password → verify your email
3
You now have a free account. No credit card needed.
Step 2 — Connect your Discogs account
💿
Connect with Discogs
Securely authorise VinylVaultPro — no password shared
Not connected — tap to connect your Discogs account
Or use a Personal Access Token
Go to discogs.com → Settings → Developers → Generate new token, then paste it below.
Your Discogs Username (needed for collection sync)
Find it at discogs.com → your profile URL: discogs.com/user/username
💱 Pricing Currency
All Discogs prices will be returned in this currency. Defaults to USD.
🎯 Preferred Price Condition
Which condition listings should pricing show? Applies to buy-sheet prices, wantlist values, and market page. "Any" shows the cheapest available copy regardless of grade.
🛒 eBay Pricing (optional fallback when Discogs is unavailable)
Free eBay developer account. Gets low/median/high from active vinyl listings. → Sign up free at developer.ebay.com
eBay App ID (Client ID) → Get from developer.ebay.com/my/keys
Paste to enable eBay prices
🎫 Events API Keys (optional — Bandsintown & Ticketmaster work without keys)
Songkick API Key → Get key
Setlist.fm API Key → Get key
Ticketmaster API Key → Get key (demo key active by default)
🔎 Deep Price Refresh
exact prices from live listings · slower
Checks real for-sale listings to find the exact cheapest buy-now price (with shipping) for every record. Most accurate, but slower — a few seconds each. For routine updates, use Sync from Discogs below (much faster).
Starting…
Requires Discogs API token · Respects rate limits · Collection safe if lookup fails
Auto-Sync from Discogs
Import new records you've added on Discogs, update market prices for existing ones, and keep both collections in sync automatically.
⚠ No token No username Never synced
Sync Now = import new records + refresh prices (fast). Update Prices Only = refresh prices, skip importing.

Where does this go?
💾 Backup & Export
📊 Export to Excel
Downloads a complete .xlsx file with 7 tabs — Collection, Sold & Traded, Wantlist, Tracklists, Price History, Spending Tracker, and Dashboard summary. All data, fully formatted.
To save to Google Drive: tap Download → when file appears in your browser downloads, tap the share icon → Save to Drive.

To save to iPhone Files / iCloud: tap Download → tap the share icon → Save to Files → choose iCloud Drive or On My iPhone.

To open in Excel or Google Sheets: open the downloaded .xlsx directly from your Files app or email it to yourself.
🎵 Export as Playlist
Find your collection on streaming services or copy a plain-text playlist to share anywhere.
Plain Text — copy & paste anywhere
💾 Backup App Data
Downloads a .json backup of everything — your collection, wantlist, sales, tracklists, price history, and colour theme. Use this to restore the app on any device.
Save to Phone: downloads the file to your phone's Downloads folder. On iPhone, tap the share icon on the downloaded file → Save to Files to move it to iCloud.

Google Drive: taps Download first, then opens Google Drive so you can upload it. Sign in to Drive if prompted.
📥 Import from Discogs CSV
Already have your collection on Discogs? Export it as a CSV and import your entire library — artist, album, label, year, condition, rating, and notes — in seconds.
📂 Restore from Backup
Pick a previously saved .json backup file to restore your data. This replaces everything currently in the app.
From iCloud / Files: tap Choose Backup File → browse to where you saved the .json file → tap it.

From Google Drive: first download the backup from Drive to your phone, then tap Choose Backup File and find it in Downloads.
🔁 Auto-Backup Reminder
This app saves your data automatically in your browser's local storage. However, local storage can be cleared if you clear your browser cache. We recommend backing up at least once a month.
Update Complete
Record-by-Record Changes
🕑 View past reports →
📷 Scan Barcode / UPC
Point camera at barcode
Or enter barcode / UPC manually
How to scan
1
Tap Start Camera and allow camera access when prompted
2
Hold the barcode on the back of the record sleeve inside the gold brackets
3
The app searches Discogs automatically when it reads the barcode
4
Pick the correct pressing and choose Add to Collection or Add to Wantlist
Tips
• Barcodes are usually on the back of the sleeve or on the inner sleeve
• Works with EAN-13 (most common) and UPC-A formats
• No Discogs token? Lookups fall back to MusicBrainz automatically — free, no account needed
• Discogs gives marketplace prices too; MusicBrainz gives full tracklists and pressing info
• No barcode? Enter the UPC manually in the field above

Spotify Import
🔑 Connect your Spotify account
Import your Saved Albums and Followed Artists directly to your wantlist.
Uses Spotify's free OAuth — no password shared with this app.
One-time setup (2 minutes)
  1. Go to developer.spotify.com/dashboard
  2. Click Create App — any name is fine
  3. Add REDIRECT_URI below as a Redirect URI in the app settings
  4. Copy your Client ID and paste it below
—
Tap to copy
✓ Connected
Albums
⏳
Loading…
⠿ Reorder Stores
Drag to set your preferred order
Text Size
More options in Style & Customise
Update from Link
💰 Prices look wrong? Pin the exact pressing.
Wildly high or low prices almost always mean this record is matched to the wrong Discogs release (a different pressing, or another record entirely). Pasting the correct release link re-pins it, and every future price pull uses your exact pressing.
1. On Discogs, open the exact pressing you own (year, label, colour, catalog #).
2. Copy that page's link — it looks like discogs.com/release/12345
3. Paste it below and tap Fetch & Extract Info.
⚠ A master link (/master/… or “All Versions”) covers every pressing at once — we'll resolve it to the main release, but for accurate pricing paste the specific release you own.

Or paste any other URL (eBay, a store page) or raw text copied from a page — the app will fetch it and extract what it can.
